headline: Open-weight coding models and local image generators headline the week as code quality and text-to-image generation break out together.
body: Two open-weight releases anchor the window: GLM-5.2 shipped as a free, fast coder through OpenCode with roughly 200 requests per five-hour window for repo exploration, bug fixes, and refactoring [0], and Krea 2 arrived as an open-source, uncensored local image generator with ComfyUI integration and LoRA fine-tuning [4]. On the technique side, code quality, text-to-image generation, noise prediction, and generative AI are all breaking out, with DiffusionBench introducing a holistic evaluation framework for generative diffusion transformers [8]. Tool integration and vector search round out the breakout set, while macro caution surfaces as tech stocks slid on AI bubble fears [9] and a Florida school board paused an educator AI rollout over privacy and security concerns [7].

cited_items.title: GLM 5.2 FULLY FREE & FAST CODER: This AI Coder is CRAZY & WORKS!
New top local AI image generator is here! Already uncensored
DiffusionBench: Towards Holistic Evaluation of Generative Diffusion Transformers
Tech stocks slump as AI bubble fears loom
Broward School Board pauses rollout of AI platform for educators amid privacy and security concerns - CBS News
